response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable individual contributor with a broad technical background spanning distributed systems, full-stack development, and some recent agentic AI tooling work. However, this is an executive CTO role requiring demonstrated engineering leadership at scale, and the resume shows no evidence of managing teams, scaling an engineering organization from 10 to 30+ people, board-level communication, or owning architectural decisions across a company. His entire career history lists individual contributor titles (Staff/Senior Software Engineer/Developer), and his fintech exposure is limited to a 15-month senior developer stint at RIDE Capital. The agentic AI experience mentioned is framed as tooling contributions rather than production multi-agent system architecture or leadership.
strengths: Long career with genuine distributed systems and microservices experience, including high-throughput work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second
Recent hands-on involvement with agentic AI tooling and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, showing awareness of the space
Polyglot engineer with open source initiative and a track record of contributing across a wide range of environments and tech stacks
concerns: Zero demonstrated engineering management experience — every role listed is an individual contributor position with no reports, team ownership, or org-scaling track record
No executive or C-suite experience of any kind: no board communication, no P&L ownership, no governance of outsourced engineering, and no strategic roadmap leadership

Agentic AI and fintech depth are both thin for this role — MCP tooling contributions and a brief fintech developer stint fall far short of 'production multi-agent systems in regulated financial environments'

key_factors.factor: No engineering leadership or management history
Individual contributor track record only — CTO role mismatch
Some distributed systems and early agentic AI tooling experience
